component.name: Button
component.description: Triggers an action or navigation. The primary way a user initiates a response from the system.

when: Confirming or cancelling a destructive operation

antiPatterns.scenario: Using the default variant more than once per view section
antiPatterns.reason: Multiple filled buttons compete for attention and collapse the visual hierarchy
antiPatterns.alternative: Use one default and demote the rest to outlined

antiPatterns.scenario: Replacing an anchor tag with a Button for page navigation
antiPatterns.reason: Buttons fire actions; links navigate. Misuse breaks keyboard and screen-reader expectations
antiPatterns.alternative: Use an <a> element styled directly — there is no ghost variant yet; do not misuse outlined as a link style

antiPatterns.scenario: Placing a text Button inside a compact space such as a data table cell
antiPatterns.reason: Text buttons need padding and create visual noise in tight layouts
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Button with the square or round variant for icon-only compact actions

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Button for a destructive action without a confirmation step
antiPatterns.reason: There is no danger variant — the default filled style does not visually signal risk
antiPatterns.alternative: Pair a default Button with a confirmation dialog or inline warning text before the action fires

purpose.default: The primary filled action on a surface. Use once per view section — multiple filled buttons collapse visual hierarchy.
purpose.outlined: Supporting or secondary actions that sit alongside a default button. Lower visual weight, still clearly interactive.

purpose.square: Icon-only action in a square container. Use for compact affordances where a text label is unnecessary (e.g. toolbar actions).
purpose.round: Icon-only action in a circular container. Use when the surrounding layout calls for a softer, pill-shaped affordance.

states.description: Keyboard focus ring visible. Must meet WCAG 3:1 contrast against the surface.
Action is unavailable. Pointer events suppressed. Must not rely on color alone — also set aria-disabled='true'.
Async action in progress. Label replaced by a spinner. Width must not collapse to prevent layout shift.

compositionPatterns.name: form-actions-row
compositionPatterns.description: Primary + secondary button pair at the bottom of a form. Primary commits, secondary cancels.

compositionPatterns.name: card-cta
compositionPatterns.description: Single call-to-action anchored to the bottom of a Card.

compositionPatterns.name: danger-confirmation
compositionPatterns.description: Destructive action paired with a cancel. Danger variant always on the right.
